summary: Phase 1 Prompt 1.5 — Projects + project_team_members full-stack review. Backend pytest suite is GREEN (93/93 test_projects passing, 135/135 incl. audit). Frontend UI flows validated end-to-end for test-pm and test-readonly: list renders with filter/search/clear, margin% gate honoured, new-project form submits and navigates to detail, ha→acres conversion works, planning expiry preview computes +3y (Full) / +2y (Reserved_Matters), detail page shows correct FORWARD_TRANSITIONS (Lead → Appraisal/Dead), override/delete hidden for non-super_admin, tabs switch cleanly. Three MINOR UX issues and one verification gap (super_admin MFA gate) documented below.

ui_bugs.issue: Acres→ha conversion is one-way-guarded: onAcresChange only writes ha when ha is currently empty (ProjectNew.jsx:70 `!form.site_area_ha`). onHaChange always overwrites acres. Result: typing ha=1.2 then changing acres to 5 leaves ha stuck at 1.2. Backend reconciles on save so data is ultimately consistent, but UI is misleading.

Read-only user (no projects.create) can still load the ProjectNew form via direct URL. The New Project button is correctly hidden in the list, but the route itself isn't permission-gated. Backend returns 403 on submit so it is not a security bug, just misleading UX. Wrap the route in a permission guard (projects.create) or bounce back to /projects.

The audit fetch (ProjectDetail.jsx:529) has no .catch(); for users without audit.view the API returns 403 and the component silently renders 'No audit events yet.' in the empty state. Should show an explicit access-denied message or hide the tab for users lacking audit.view.

action_items: UX fix: make acres→ha conversion symmetric with ha→acres in ProjectNew.jsx (onAcresChange should always recompute site_area_ha).
UX fix: gate the /projects/new route with projects.create permission (or redirect to /projects when missing). Currently read-only users can load the form.

UX fix: ProjectDetail AuditTab should handle 403 explicitly (e.g., .catch on api.get('/audit', ...)) and render a distinct 'You do not have permission to view audit events for this project' message instead of the zero-state.

Optional: consider giving project_manager role audit.view_own (scoped to their entities) so they can see project_audit trails for projects they manage — today PM sees the same empty state as any other unauthorised caller.
critical_code_review_comments: ProjectNew.jsx: area conversion is asymmetric (bug above). Also note that both `site_area_ha` and `site_area_acres` are sent to POST — backend services.projects.reconcile_area handles precedence, confirmed by 93 passing tests.
ProjectNew.jsx:100 coerces units_target with parseInt — good. Empty-string stripping on line 96–98 is defensive.
ProjectDetail.jsx allowedNext correctly pulls from FORWARD_TRANSITIONS (verified Lead → Appraisal|Dead render).
ProjectDetail.jsx:165 — isSuper gating on Override button is correct (super_admin only per spec).
ProjectDetail.jsx:172 canDelete gate: delete-project-button was hidden for test-pm, which matches backend service has_project_dependents blocking and RBAC requiring projects.delete — good.

AdvanceStageModal: disables submit until reason.trim() present when target=Dead (line 637). Good. But note the modal doesn't enforce a minimum length on dead_reason client-side — backend enforces via Pydantic, so it will just 422 on short reasons. Minor — add min-length hint.
OverrideStageModal: canSubmit requires reason.trim().length >= 10 (line 658) — matches backend min 10-char enforcement. Good.
ProjectsList: search form has no visible submit button — relies on Enter. Consider adding a submit icon button for discoverability; not blocking.
File sizes: ProjectDetail.jsx is 788 lines — above the 700-line guideline. Consider splitting AdvanceStageModal / OverrideStageModal / TeamTab / AuditTab into separate files.

success_rate.frontend: 90% — all primary flows validated for PM + read_only. Super_admin-gated override modal not browser-tested (MFA gate). Three MINOR UX issues logged.

test_credentials: See /app/memory/test_credentials.md. Browser tests used test-pm@example.test and test-readonly@example.test (both non-MFA). Backend pytest uses login_with_auto_enroll helper for MFA-enforced roles.
seed_data_creation: Created one project 'TEST QA Project {ts}' via the UI during detail-page verification. Attempted SQL cleanup afterwards; backend may have already cleaned via cascades. No lasting seed data added.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Prompt 1.5 is functionally GREEN. The three MINOR frontend UX items (acres→ha symmetry, /projects/new route gating, audit 403 swallowing) can be fixed and self-verified by the main agent without full re-test. Super_admin override modal (data-testid=override-stage-button, override-submit, override-reason) was not exercised in the browser because test-admin triggers the MFA TOTP challenge; backend pytest already exercises it comprehensively in test_projects.py::TestStageOverride. If you need browser coverage for super_admin flows, use pyotp to drive the TOTP challenge or pre-seed an MFA-bypassed admin. Also note: ProjectDetail.jsx is 788 lines — consider splitting for maintainability.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no functional bugs in Prompt 1.5. Only three UX asymmetries and one oversized file (see comments).
